markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Verify proof does not rely on external apps, CDNs, manually launched servers, Docker-only paths, or undeclared daemons. This MT belongs to SecurityPortabilityValidation. Prove WP-009 behavior with negative fixtures, visual debugging, portability checks, and validator-ready evidence.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
Validation must include negative fixtures for false PASS, stale state, missing Postgres, missing assets, secret leakage, partial PDF extraction, parallel write conflicts, and UI overlap.
Portability checks must reject hardcoded machine paths and authority stored only in generated projections.
scope.expected_behavior: ExternalDependencyTripwireFixture leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: ExternalDependencyTripwireFixture is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

evidence.summary: MT-225..229 add real secret/path leakage negative fixtures, portable path redaction/validation including wrappers and Windows drive roots, Postgres-only storage fail-closed behavior, Node/Rust forbidden-source tripwires, actual allowlist authority, and fail-closed scanner read-error handling.

commands.result: PASS; 3/3 source tripwire unit tests passed
PASS; 9/9 dependency policy checks passed, forbidden-source-tripwire clean

evidence.summary: Reviewer attacked detector drift, wrapper/delimiter path leaks, bare drive roots, UNC/POSIX paths, Node/Rust tripwire split-brain, broad source exceptions, read-error skipping, path-only probes, SQLite fallback/default behavior, and mock-only proof; no blockers found.

wp_validator_review_v2.review_method: Integration Validator multi-agent workflow (wf_0ac25815-485 + wf_98386044-70a): independent per-MT reviewer + adversarial confirm on FAIL. No ACP. Governance/repo paperwork non-blocking per operator directive (2026-06-18). Bar = good code vs MT contract + WP packet + current Master Spec v02.194, plus the WP_VALIDATOR Spec-Realism Gate.
wp_validator_review_v2.summary: MT-229 requires an ExternalDependencyTripwireFixture: a concrete, restartable negative-fixture proof that WP-009 behavior rejects external apps, CDNs, unmanaged daemons, Docker-default paths, and SQLite as proof/authority. The committed code delivers a real source-tripwire scanner (Rust + Node), a machine-readable allowlist declaring the forbidden classes, and negative-fixture tests that exercise the real scanner against the real allowlist and shell out to the real Node validator. Implements the MT.
findings: MT-229 dedicated test mt_229_external_dependency_tripwire_rejects_unmanaged_proof_defaults (security_portability_validation_tests.rs:480-527) feeds six realistic forbidden-default fixtures (redis://, docker compose up, cdn.jsdelivr.net Monaco worker, photoshop.exe, ollama serve + localhost:11434, npm run dev + localhost:5173) through the real scan_source_text and asserts all four required classes trip: outside_server_daemon, outside_app, docker_default, cdn_runtime_asset. Real resource touch, not a mock.

source_tripwires.rs (new, 452 lines) is a genuine scanner: scan_source_text (line 222) matches both built-in patterns and allowlist-declared source_scan_patterns over path+content; assert_source_tripwire_policy_for_files (line 129) loads the real allowlist via RuntimeDependencyAllowlist::load_from_repo_root and scans real files. No stub returns.

runtime_dependency_allowlist.json declares every class MT-229 asserts: cdn_runtime_asset with cdn.jsdelivr.net/unpkg/esm.sh (lines 71-85), docker_default with 'docker compose'/'docker run'/docker.sock (57-62), outside_app photoshop.exe (43-47), outside_server_daemon redis/ollama/dev-server patterns (50-54). This is the real authority data the test consumes.

mt_228_229_node_dependency_policy_validator_file_probe_fails_closed (line 604) and _missing_file_probe (line 568) actually spawn Command::new('node') against the real app/scripts/check-dependency-policy.mjs with --source-tripwire-files and assert non-zero exit plus forbidden-source-tripwire / file_read_errors reporting. The node script genuinely implements --source-tripwire-files (line 67), the forbidden-source-tripwire check id, and fail-closed logic pass = violations.length===0 && allReadErrors.length===0 (line 200). Real cross-runtime proof.

No deferred-live escapes: grep for todo!()/unimplemented!()/unreachable!()/'pending MT-'/'deferred to follow'/*Unavailable variants across the dependency_policy module, the integration test, and both node scripts returned zero hits.

Exceptions are exact path+class+pattern scoped (source_tripwire_exceptions, lines 181-238) and the test honors_exact_pattern_scoped_source_tripwire_exceptions (source_tripwires.rs:423) proves the same pattern outside the exact exception path still fails closed - no broad escape. scan_self_exempt_paths was explicitly narrowed from a blanket substring exemption to exact paths (allowlist H3 note, lines 164-179).

implementation_commit 9915fcfa confirmed ancestor of feat/WP-KERNEL-009 HEAD; the 12-file diff (1707 insertions) is real product code under allowed paths (src/backend/handshake_core, app/src, app/scripts). No /.GOV/ or product-governance boundary spill. No forbidden SQLite/external-search-engine/CDN-worker dependency introduced - they are actively tripwired against.

Aside (non-blocking): MT contract uses generic field names (owned_files/spec_anchors not literally present; fields are relevant_files/hbr_focus) and cites no implementation_notes; this is paperwork-shape only and per ground rules is not a failing reason. The durable-authority (PostgreSQL-only) claim is owned by sibling MT-227 (mt_227 test at line 377 proves ControlPlaneStorageConfig::resolve fails closed without SQLite fallback); MT-229 is the negative-tripwire half, correctly scoped.
evidence_checked: grep sweep for placeholder/deferred-live escape patterns across impl + tests + node scripts (zero hits)

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
